name: Alertmanager
summary: Companion to Prometheus that routes, groups, deduplicates, and silences alerts and dispatches them to email, Slack, PagerDuty, and more.
description: Quenchworks hardened Prometheus Alertmanager (handles alerts from Prometheus - dedup, grouping, routing to email/Slack/PagerDuty/webhook, silences, inhibition). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; no auth, NetworkPolicy is the boundary.

name: Atlantis
summary: Terraform pull-request automation that runs plan on PRs and apply from PR comments, enforcing reviewed, Git-driven infrastructure changes with state locking.
description: Quenchworks hardened Atlantis (Terraform/OpenTofu pull-request automation). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; bundles OpenTofu (MPL-2.0, default execution engine) instead of Terraform. StatefulSet with a persistent data dir for cloned repos and plans; server + webhook + UI on 4141.

name: Caddy
summary: Web server and reverse proxy with fully automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt and a simple Caddyfile config.
description: Quenchworks hardened Caddy web server with automatic HTTPS. Minimal, nonroot, read-only-rootfs, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Cassandra
summary: Distributed wide-column NoSQL store built for linear horizontal scale and high availability with no single point of failure, tuned for heavy writes.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache Cassandra. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: ClickHouse
summary: Column-oriented OLAP database for real-time analytical queries over very large datasets, with high ingest rates and fast aggregations.
description: Quenchworks hardened ClickHouse. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. Single-node; ships ClickHouse's official self-contained static binary.

name: CockroachDB
summary: Distributed SQL database with PostgreSQL wire compatibility and automatic horizontal scaling and survivability. BUSL is source-available, not OSI-approved; no clean in-catalog substitute yet, evaluate PostgreSQL for single-region SQL.
description: CAUTION/BSL-1.1 (NOT open source): Quenchworks hardened CockroachDB, distributed SQL (Postgres wire). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. Single-node insecure by default; secure/multi-node are follow-ups.
cleanAlternative: No in-catalog clean substitute yet — evaluate PostgreSQL for single-region SQL.

name: coolify
summary: Hardened coolify image, built from source on Wolfi.
description: Quenchworks hardened Coolify (self-hostable PaaS / Heroku & Netlify alternative). A single umbrella chart wiring the minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE Coolify control-plane images (pinned by digest) — the PHP/Laravel app (php-fpm + nginx + Horizon + scheduler under supervisord) and the realtime tier (soketi + terminal bridge) — over bundled Quenchworks PostgreSQL 15 and Redis. Coolify manages EXTERNAL Docker hosts over SSH; the privileged helper/proxy build tier runs on those managed hosts, NOT in this cluster.

name: CouchDB
summary: Document database with an HTTP/JSON API and multi-master replication, designed for offline-first sync across nodes and devices.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache CouchDB 3.x. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. QuickJS view server (no SpiderMonkey); Fauxton not bundled.

name: DocumentDB
summary: Self-contained MongoDB-compatible server bundling PostgreSQL, the DocumentDB extension, and a wire gateway. Linux Foundation project, truly open under MIT.
description: Quenchworks hardened DocumentDB — a self-contained MongoDB-compatible server (PostgreSQL 17 + documentdb extension + pg_documentdb_gw gateway in one container). Nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Dragonfly
summary: Multi-threaded, Redis- and Memcached-compatible in-memory datastore built to scale vertically on one node. BUSL is source-available, not open source; prefer Valkey (BSD-3-Clause).
description: Quenchworks hardened Dragonfly (Redis/Memcached-compatible in-memory datastore). WARNING: BSL-1.1, NOT open source (converts to Apache-2.0 years after release) — prefer Valkey (BSD), the clean alternative. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Elasticsearch
summary: Distributed search and analytics engine. Shipped under SSPL/Elastic License, which are not OSI-approved; OpenSearch (Apache-2.0) is the open drop-in fork.
description: Quenchworks hardened Elasticsearch on a minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. CAUTION: default distribution is SSPL-1.0/Elastic License 2.0 -- NOT OSI open source; OpenSearch (Apache-2.0) is the clean drop-in alternative.
cleanAlternative: OpenSearch (Apache-2.0) — the open drop-in fork of Elasticsearch.

name: etcd
summary: Distributed, strongly consistent key-value store using Raft consensus. The backing store for Kubernetes and a building block for service coordination and config.
description: Quenchworks hardened etcd. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: FerretDB
summary: MongoDB-compatible document database that translates the MongoDB wire protocol onto PostgreSQL via the DocumentDB extension. The clean-license substitute for MongoDB.
description: Quenchworks hardened FerretDB (MongoDB-compatible proxy). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Fluent Bit
summary: Lightweight, fast log and metrics processor and forwarder for collecting, filtering, and shipping telemetry to many backends.
description: Quenchworks hardened Fluent Bit log/metrics processor and shipper in deployment (gateway) mode. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; receive logs in, ship logs out, configured by a templated config.

name: Garage
summary: Lightweight, S3-compatible object store for small, self-hosted, geo-distributed deployments that stays available across node failures. Licensed AGPL; runs well on modest hardware.
description: Quenchworks hardened Garage (S3-compatible object store), single node. The AGPL alternative to the default SeaweedFS object store. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Gitea
summary: Lightweight self-hosted Git service with repositories, issues, pull requests, and built-in CI/CD (Actions). A low-footprint GitHub alternative.
description: Quenchworks hardened Gitea (lightweight self-hosted Git service). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; runs against PostgreSQL (bundled Quenchworks PostgreSQL by default).

name: Grafana
summary: Dashboards and visualization for metrics, logs, and traces across many data sources. OSS edition, licensed AGPL.
description: Quenchworks hardened Grafana OSS (observability dashboards). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; pairs with the Quenchworks Prometheus and VictoriaMetrics datasources.

name: HAProxy
summary: High-performance TCP and HTTP load balancer and reverse proxy known for reliability and fine-grained traffic control at scale.
description: Quenchworks hardened HAProxy TCP/HTTP load balancer. Minimal, nonroot, read-only-rootfs, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: harbor
summary: Hardened harbor image, built from source on Wolfi.
description: Quenchworks hardened Harbor (cloud-native OCI registry with RBAC, replication, vulnerability scanning and signing). A single umbrella chart wiring seven minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE component images (pinned by digest) — core, jobservice, registry, registryctl, portal, the optional Trivy scan adapter and metrics exporter — over the Quenchworks PostgreSQL and Valkey charts.

name: Harbor observability
summary: Hardened harbor-observability image, built from source on Wolfi.
description: Quenchworks Harbor observability stack — a Harbor-style monitoring meta-chart that deploys the Quenchworks Prometheus and Grafana charts preconfigured to monitor a Harbor instance. Prometheus scrapes the harbor-exporter (plus core/registry/jobservice /metrics); Grafana boots with a Prometheus datasource and a provisioned Harbor dashboard (request rate, registry/core health, GC, quota, task queue). Nonroot, 0-CVE images pinned by digest via the sub-charts.

name: InfluxDB
summary: Time-series database purpose-built for ingesting and querying metrics, events, and IoT/sensor data at high write rates.
description: Quenchworks hardened InfluxDB 2.x. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Kafka
summary: Distributed event-streaming platform for high-throughput, durable, replayable publish-subscribe pipelines and stream processing.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache Kafka (KRaft). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Keycloak
summary: Open-source identity and access management server providing SSO, user federation, and OAuth2/OIDC and SAML for apps and APIs.
description: Quenchworks hardened Keycloak (identity & access management — SSO, OIDC, SAML). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; runs the OPTIMIZED Quarkus build against PostgreSQL.

name: Loki
summary: Horizontally scalable log aggregation system from Grafana that indexes only labels, not full log text. Like Prometheus, but for logs. Licensed AGPL.
description: Quenchworks hardened Grafana Loki (single-binary log aggregation system). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; auth disabled, NetworkPolicy is the boundary. Grafana is its UI.

name: MariaDB
summary: Community-developed relational database and drop-in MySQL successor, GPL-licensed and fully open. Default MySQL-compatible engine for the catalog.
description: Quenchworks hardened MariaDB. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Meilisearch
summary: Fast, typo-tolerant full-text search engine with an instant-search API, easy to embed for site and in-app search with relevant results out of the box.
description: Quenchworks hardened Meilisearch (fast, typo-tolerant full-text search engine) with the bundled mini-dashboard. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; single-node StatefulSet, mandatory master-key auth (MEILI_ENV=production) backed by a generated Secret.

name: Memcached
summary: Simple, high-performance distributed in-memory cache for storing small objects and database query results to reduce backend load.
description: Quenchworks hardened memcached. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: MongoDB
summary: Document (NoSQL) database for JSON-like data. SSPL is not OSI-approved / source-available, not open source; prefer the clean alternative FerretDB or DocumentDB.
description: Quenchworks hardened MongoDB Community Server. LICENSE: SSPL-1.0 — NOT OSI-approved, NOT open source (OSI declined the SSPL); caution tier. Clean alternatives: QuenchWorks FerretDB + documentdb (MongoDB-wire-compatible, truly open) — prefer them. Nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.
cleanAlternative: FerretDB + DocumentDB — MongoDB-wire-compatible and truly open (Apache-2.0 / PostgreSQL).

name: MySQL
summary: Widely used open-source relational database for general-purpose OLTP workloads, with broad framework and tooling support.
description: Quenchworks hardened MySQL. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: NATS
summary: Lightweight, high-performance messaging system for cloud-native pub/sub and request-reply, with optional JetStream persistence and streaming.
description: Quenchworks hardened NATS server. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Neo4j
summary: Graph database for highly connected data, queried with Cypher for traversals and relationship-heavy workloads. Community edition is GPLv3.
description: Quenchworks hardened Neo4j Community Edition (GPLv3), single node. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: nginx
summary: High-performance web server, reverse proxy, and load balancer for serving static content and fronting application backends.
description: Quenchworks hardened nginx web server / reverse proxy. Minimal, nonroot, read-only-rootfs, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: OpenBao
summary: Open-source secrets and encryption management for tokens, keys, and certificates, with dynamic secrets and leasing. The Linux Foundation MPL-2.0 community fork of HashiCorp Vault.
description: Quenchworks hardened OpenBao (secrets management; the truly-open MPL-2.0 fork of HashiCorp Vault). Single-node raft integrated storage on a minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. A real node boots SEALED + UNINITIALIZED — init/unseal is an operator step.

name: OpenSearch
summary: Search and analytics suite with a Kibana-style dashboards UI. The Apache-2.0 community fork of Elasticsearch and the recommended open alternative.
description: Quenchworks hardened OpenSearch. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: OpenTelemetry Collector
summary: OpenTelemetry Collector that receives, processes, and exports traces, metrics, and logs, a vendor-neutral pipeline for telemetry routing.
description: Quenchworks hardened OpenTelemetry Collector (contrib distribution) in gateway mode. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; vendor-agnostic traces/metrics/logs pipeline configured by a templated config.

name: pgbouncer
summary: Lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL that fronts thousands of client connections with a small backend pool, cutting per-connection overhead.
description: Quenchworks hardened PgBouncer — lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; pools client connections in front of a bundled or external PostgreSQL backend.

name: PostgreSQL
summary: Advanced open-source relational database (v18.x) with strong SQL, ACID transactions, JSON, and a rich extension ecosystem. The catalog's default SQL engine.
description: Quenchworks hardened PostgreSQL. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: PostgreSQL + DocumentDB
summary: PostgreSQL 17 plus the open DocumentDB extension, providing BSON document storage and queries. The storage backend that powers FerretDB v2.
description: Quenchworks hardened PostgreSQL 17 + DocumentDB extension (FerretDB v2 backend). Nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Prometheus
summary: Metrics collection, storage, and alerting system that scrapes targets and runs PromQL queries. The de-facto cloud-native monitoring TSDB.
description: Quenchworks hardened Prometheus (single-node metrics server / TSDB). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; no auth, NetworkPolicy is the boundary.

name: Pulsar
summary: Cloud-native distributed messaging and streaming platform with multi-tenancy, geo-replication, and tiered storage that separates compute from storage.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache Pulsar. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. Single-node standalone (broker + in-process BookKeeper + embedded RocksDB metadata in one JVM); multi-node cluster is a follow-up.

name: Qdrant
summary: Vector database and similarity-search engine for AI embeddings, powering semantic search, recommendations, and RAG with filtered nearest-neighbor queries.
description: Quenchworks hardened Qdrant (high-performance vector database / similarity search). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; single-node by default with optional API-key auth, NetworkPolicy is the boundary.

name: RabbitMQ
summary: Reliable message broker speaking AMQP plus MQTT and STOMP, with flexible routing, queues, and acknowledgements for decoupling services.
description: Quenchworks hardened RabbitMQ. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Redis
summary: In-memory key-value store used as a cache, message broker, and ephemeral datastore. Shipped under AGPL (not permissive); Valkey is the BSD-licensed open alternative.
description: Quenchworks hardened Redis. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: RustFS
summary: S3-compatible, high-performance object store written in Rust with a zero-master architecture, positioned as an Apache-2.0 MinIO alternative. Beta/preview.
description: Quenchworks hardened RustFS (Rust, S3-compatible object storage), single node. BETA/preview — RustFS has no stable release yet. The newest S3-compatible MinIO-alternative; SeaweedFS is the default Quenchworks object store. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: ScyllaDB
summary: High-throughput, low-latency wide-column store, API-compatible with Apache Cassandra and DynamoDB. This 6.x build is the final OSS line under AGPL.
description: Quenchworks hardened ScyllaDB Open Source (Cassandra-compatible). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. AGPL-3.0. Single-node by default; multi-node via SCYLLA_SEEDS is a follow-up.

name: SeaweedFS
summary: Fast distributed storage for blobs, objects, and files (S3 API) built on Facebook's Haystack design, with O(1) disk seeks for billions of small files. Default object store after MinIO restricted its community edition.
description: Quenchworks hardened SeaweedFS (S3-compatible object storage), single node. The default Quenchworks object store. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Solr
summary: Enterprise search platform built on Apache Lucene, offering full-text search, faceting, and indexing over large document collections.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache Solr. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest. Single-node standalone (SolrCloud is a follow-up).

name: Tempo
summary: Distributed tracing backend from Grafana that ingests OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, and Zipkin spans and stores them cheaply in object storage. Licensed AGPL.
description: Quenchworks hardened Grafana Tempo (single-binary distributed tracing backend). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; OTLP receivers on 4317/4318, no auth, NetworkPolicy is the boundary. Grafana is its UI.

name: Temporal
summary: Durable workflow orchestration engine that persists execution state so long-running, multi-step processes survive crashes and resume exactly where they left off.
description: Quenchworks hardened Temporal (durable workflow execution engine / orchestration server). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; runs the single-binary all-in-one server (frontend + history + matching + worker) against PostgreSQL.

name: Traefik
summary: Cloud-native reverse proxy and load balancer with automatic service discovery, dynamic config, and built-in Let's Encrypt TLS.
description: Quenchworks hardened Traefik cloud-native edge router / reverse proxy and Kubernetes ingress controller. Minimal, nonroot, read-only-rootfs, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Valkey
summary: BSD-licensed in-memory key-value store, the truly-open community fork of Redis 7.2. QuenchWorks' default cache and the recommended Redis drop-in replacement.
description: Quenchworks hardened Valkey. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: Vector
summary: High-performance observability pipeline that collects, transforms, and routes logs, metrics, and traces between sources and sinks.
description: Quenchworks hardened Vector (high-performance observability data pipeline for logs/metrics) in aggregator mode. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest; sources/transforms/sinks configured by a templated config, GraphQL/health API on 8686.

name: VictoriaMetrics
summary: Fast, cost-efficient time-series database that speaks PromQL and Prometheus remote_write. Drop-in long-term storage or full replacement for Prometheus.
description: Quenchworks hardened VictoriaMetrics (single-node TSDB). Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.

name: ZooKeeper
summary: Centralized coordination service for distributed systems, providing configuration, naming, leader election, and synchronization primitives.
description: Quenchworks hardened Apache ZooKeeper. Minimal, nonroot, 0-CVE image pinned by digest.
